title: The Window Is Twelve Years Wide. After That, the Damage Is Done.
description: A landmark 2026 study tracked 218 children from birth to age 12 and identified the specific developmental window during which PFAS exposure most devastates bone density — with effect sizes rivaling diet, exercise, and genetics. With 158 million Americans drinking PFAS-contaminated water, this isn't a niche environmental story. It's a population-scale skeletal health crisis that won't show up as fractures for another thirty years.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Window Is Twelve Years Wide. After That, the Damage Is Done.
